author: hangleang
content: I'm sure Grandine emit `payload_attestation` SSE, but the spec expect `payload_attestation_message` as the topic. that could explain we are missing the event in the metric

author: lqzic
content:
Hello <@680234424037670912>, while inspecting Teku's `head_v2` emission path I noticed that `head` and `head_v2` events are logically bundled together - which results in a duplicate v1 `head` emission when the head's payload status changes.
https://github.com/Consensys/teku/blob/c905b16ae7f08634793f318442b5c0cbea2d41d3/data/beaconrestapi/src/main/java/tech/pegasys/teku/beaconrestapi/handlers/v1/events/EventSubscriptionManager.java#L142-L190
I'm working on the `head_v2` implementation in Lodestar, which is why I was taking a look at your implementation.

author: lqzic
content: putting here the `head_v2` implementation question for Teku & Prysm so it doesn't get lost

author: hangleang
content: we should only emit `head_v2` when head's payload status change from `empty` to `full`.  however, should we emit the event as well when payload status change from `full` to `empty`?

author: lqzic
content: I think that Teku already emits a new event on every payload status change. I suppose that these events will be helpful to the builder entity more than the VCs. If that is the behavior we want then beacon-APIs description of head_v2 should get updated accordingly.

author: nflaig
content: the only way a payload can be reorged is by the next beacon block, meaning the head would change, imo it's questionable to me to even have the `payload_status` as part of the event at all, but I would like to hear more opinions why this is useful, and also how others have implemented this

author: nflaig
content: <@921369499733725205> is representing lodestar on this matter, he started working on an implementation

author: .paulharris
content: that'll be my bad, i probably wasn't fully across the subtlety there

author: .paulharris
content: i'll read up and see whats expected

author: .paulharris
content: iirc, if its firing twice then its changed its head - we've got two fork choice nodes so the head literally changes so maybe its just how it is... has anyone implemented that differently?

author: lqzic
content: Well, the only other available implementation atm is Prysm's. From what I've seen, they emit both events on head update, but only `head_v2` on payload import - which makes sense because head v1 can't reflect a payload status change anyways and that also prevents event duplication.
